ActionItems: Open the Central Avenue lease-renewal conversation early
Track opening-night conversion for the next two exhibitions
Weigh weekend floor coverage against the sales lift before the museum show
BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Monthly break-even for Modern Edge Gallery before the Central Avenue lease renewal and the fall museum-show season; commission volume and floor coverage are the swing factors.

CurrentState.Note: Gallery operations only; excludes Kristy's personal ceramic sales and the Nob Hill casita mortgage.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: The gallery breaks even at roughly 8 artwork sales per month at the 50% commission split. At ~9 sales it runs a thin margin, so protecting collector relationships and opening-night conversion matters more than trimming costs.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $750/mo if property tax passes through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/member, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 → 30 members minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 55 members.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to $850/mo (unlikely per Central Avenue Properties)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. The landlord said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.
